name: Persia (state flag, 1907–1933)

colors.symbolism: the crowned Lion and Sun emblem

description: A green–white–red horizontal tricolour with a gold Lion and Sun (Shir-o-Khorshid) on the white band, the sun bearing a face and the emblem surmounted by a crown — the state version of the Persian flag.
history: State flag of Persia codified by the Supplementary Fundamental Laws of 7 October 1907, with emblem details fixed by the decree of 4 September 1910. The pale colours and the sun's facial features were retained until 1933, when Reza Shah darkened the colours and removed the sun's face.

features.role: lion of the Shir-o-Khorshid holding a sword
